lines.text: Jiu? That name sounds familiar.
Oh, he could be Sixi's son.
Let's go find him and talk.
His family must not know he's skipping class.
Go talk to Wulü. Maybe he knows something.
Jiyan the General, Jinhsi the Magistrate, and Jué, Jinzhou’s Sentinel... Is there any kind of connection between them?
And she's gone... Huh, that was fast.
(Better hide here for now.)
Looking for me?
It's getting closer.
Maybe we'll come across some that look like you, a whole pack of them.
(That message... I think I should trust it.)
(Rinascita? I may finally learn who Abby really is...)
Opening a portal will drain your computing power. I think it's better served for Black Shores operations.
(That message... I think I should trust it.)
(Rinascita? I may finally learn who Abby really is...)
Opening a portal will drain your computing power. I think it's better served for Black Shores operations.
(That message... I think I should trust it.)
(Rinascita? I may finally learn who Abby really is...)
Opening a portal will drain your computing power. I think it's better served for Black Shores operations.
(The Sentinel has countless ways to send a message, and through the Order is apparently the most convenient one, but why did they go through all the trouble...?)
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: (Maybe I'm wrong. Maybe calling for help was the only option?)
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: (With that message left on Tethys… Could the Sentinel here be in a situation similar to Jué's?)
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: (But it seems like the locals haven't laid eyes on their Sentinel. Everything feels off. That Echo might be our only lead right now.)
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: That's enough, Abby.

lines.text: Have you seen this before?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Lorelei?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: We have to do something.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: You're…?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Me?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: I didn't catch your name.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: It's the Fractsidus. I've dealt with them before, and I've seen a flower just like this.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Wait. The Sentinel? Does that mean...?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Can you contact Imperator? I need to meet with the Sentinel.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: One last thing. Those oathbreakers wearing Masks of Madness you mentioned, do you know who they are?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Conductor...
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: In any case, we managed to stop their plot in time and found the missing Vitreum Dancer.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Speaking of which, are all Primuses called the <ano=Sentinel's Resonator>Enlightened One</ano>?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: The stela we saw mentioned the Sentinel, and we're in the ruins of the seminary.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: All the clues we've gathered so far point to the Threnodian.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: And the Fisalia family worships the Threnodian. We'll have to wait for Carlotta to arrange an audience with them.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Something tells me this Carnevale is just the beginning. There's more ahead, I'm sure of it.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: There are still many unresolved questions regarding the Order and the Sentinel. We need time to sort through what we've gathered.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Rushing into action now might only alert the Order's spies and disrupt what we've worked so hard to protect.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Alright, let's go. We should also keep an eye out for anything unusual along the way.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: (He said she "once again resonated with the Sentinel," was that really a Second Resonance Awakening? Sentinel Jué had a trial with Jinhsi, but my permission was needed to transfer the Sentinel's power...)
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: These Echoes... Are they guarding the vault?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: And you.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: The Order must have been behind it, right?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: What is it?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: There's only one explanation. These are not fabrications but buried truth.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Watch out!

lines.text: …Carlotta?

lines.text: This is the research outpost you mentioned before?
Aemeath...
Aemeath.
You don't always have to smile through everything.
(Just like before. The moment it appears, my frequency starts getting sucked out of me.)
(Aemeath said it's a Threnodian derivative, like the Voidworm. They share similar frequencies, but… my gut says it's different.)
You did great, Abby... Go get some rest.
You went inside the Threnodian itself?
That was Aemeath. Given how well you know me, I assume you know her, too.
I brought her back to the Academy. It's a long story. I'll fill you in when there's time.
It was returned to the Exostrider.
Alright, I'll be careful.
(Aemeath still isn't back, but if my hunch is right...)
Aemeath, have you been out terrorizing those <te href=750041>Snowfluff Seals</te> again? They scatter the moment they see you.
Keep this up, and it won't be long before there isn't anyone left in the frostlands to play with...
Have you finished your lessons? You start school soon. Maybe spend less time gaming, hm?
You mean... the paper plane?
Hehe, of course there's a trick to it.
I'll teach you. But first, you finish your lessons. Then we'll fold planes together, alright?
I promise. Cross my heart.
My past self saved you...
Why did you keep this from me?
Since I woke in the Gorges of Spirits, I've met many people from my past. They've helped me recover pieces of myself. It's a good thing.
I can make my own choices. You should have a little more faith in me, Aemeath.
We will face it together, Aemeath.
You really do care about the people of Lahai-Roi, don't you, Aemeath?
Really? But the way I see it...
Go on then, Aemeath. I want to hear you sing.
You really do care about the people of Lahai-Roi, don't you, Aemeath?
Really? But the way I see it...
Go on then, Aemeath. I want to hear you sing.
(It touched the talisman then ran away. Is it trying to lead me somewhere?)
(That direction… it's the Synchrolink Training Grounds.)
...Aemeath?
(Aemeath said someone lifted her from the lake when she was a child. That's when she lost her talisman.)
(It wasn't my past self who saved her… it was me. Right here, right now.)
(My failure to save Aemeath… has it already become part of Lahai-Roi's chain of cause and effect?)
The Threnodian here needs to be dealt with first.
Yes. There's something I have to do.
The Academy will look after you. Or you can go back to live in Bjartr Woods.
...I don't know, Aemeath. I'm sorry.
But I'll come back to see you when I can. I promise.
Promise.
Don't feel you have to. The world is vast. Don't let me or Lahai-Roi hold you back.
Aemeath—
I know you're worried.
Look, Aemeath...
I've walked through lands torn apart by the Lament. Every tear weighs me down, and every cry breaks a bit of my heart.
Their pain is no different from mine. Neither is their joy.
So if I can help, I will. I have to.
You... protect me?
Hehe...
Alright, alright, I'll stop now. I know you're serious. And I'm listening to every word.
I'll be counting on you then, Aemeath.
The frequency Sigillum took is coming back. It's… not a great feeling. Abby's already helping me recover through the Tacet Mark. I'll be fine soon.
Aemeath!
(She's gone… This is the piece I was missing. Luuk said the Core's self-diagnostics would trigger peak Void Storm season.)
(She knew. When she returned the Core, she already knew. She's been waiting for this moment… waiting to travel back in time.)
(The lake in the frostlands… the Voidmatter is densest at its depths. If I'm lucky, I can find a way into Voidspace from there.)
(I can still catch up to her. I have to move, now!)
(There's still a way. I can't give up here.)
This is... her paper plane.
You can keep it, if you'd like.
I know you're not Aemeath. You're only a piece of what she left behind, kept alive by this talisman.
But if you exist... it means she's still alive, too. Doesn't it?
Otherwise, the Soliskin couldn't have brought you back.
So… it wasn't for nothing. If I hadn't gone looking for Aemeath, I never would have found this talisman.
And you wouldn't be here at all.
Luuk told me you're a piece of her. A thread that might still tie her to this world.
If I follow it… maybe I can find where she is.
He might've only said that to comfort me. No one but her has ever seen what's beyond the Stridergate. It's all just guesswork.
How long has it been for her since she was pulled into that chaos...?
Is she still alive? And if she is… how much longer can she hold on?
…I don't know.
But I will hold on to hope.
Because the world she saved is so full of it. So peaceful, and so beautiful.
It's strange… seeing "Aemeath" so quiet like this.
I will cross the Stridergate and bring you home.
Wait for me, Aemeath.
No "glitch" here either.
The Rangers back at the supply station wanted to make sure you were safe. I'm here to help you with the investigation.
No. They don't look like they belong to any of the nobles either.
(Given Grisoni's current frequency strength, I wouldn't be surprised if it returns to the Nexus right this moment… It's holding on through willpower, like a human.)
To Ragunna.
I can't say for sure, but I can share with you a true story...
(A ring, she wrote... Should be this ring-shaped ancient structure, I guess?)
Next, hanging eaves... If we look from here... Is it that pavilion?
That must be the place, then.
I’ll get going.
Those tokens from Jinhsi... It was your idea, was it not?
Traces of excavation on the ground... Looks like someone was trying to dig a hole with their bare hands. Could it be Wuli?
I know you have already looked past death, but remember... There are people who care about you. They want you to live on and be happy.
(Sounds a lot like Aalto...)
(This one's about me and Yangyang...)
(Go inside a story... Is Encore using some kind of metaphor? Doesn't hurt to check it out I guess.)
According to the treasure map, the sword should be around here somewhere.
Let's make this quick, Encore.
Did it also appear in other Wooly stories?
I know just the place.
This should be the shield mentioned in the story.
I remember Cloudy said that if you ever find yourself in a pickle in real life, you can always go back to the Wooly Kingdom to search for clues.
You also mentioned that's where the Gateway of Wishes is hidden, right? And you've always wanted to finish writing the story of the Wooly Warrior.
This means the final stage of the story should be set inside the Wooly Kingdom.
I got it. Looks like we just need to place the treasure map on top.
"The midnight chime has faded, yet the night endures. Restless hearts long for an understanding that should be ours."
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: "In the freest place of Ragunna, I await your arrival..." This should be the place Capollo mentioned in his invitation.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Hm, to locate me so precisely and deliver the invitation right into my hands... this Capollo Montelli knows what he's doing.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: (Given Capollo's stature, a private meeting at this hour... is it really just about gratitude?)
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: (Carlotta's not here yet. Guess I'll have to wait.)
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: You've been looking at this painting for a while. Anything special about it?
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: I understand decorum is important to the Montellis. But among friends, can't we be a bit more... informal?
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Why go back to what you claim to have left in the past? Unless there's something you can't ignore.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Carlotta told me Mr. Pierre always follows her grandfather around. My instincts weren't wrong when I saw you together.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: It also explains why you seemed familiar when we first met.

lines.text: (These sounds...)
I only heard strange sounds. Tell me, what direction did the Lioness just look toward?
(Before meeting Buling, when I touched the Echo in that village, I felt... a layered, indistinct frequency.)
(It was like what Abby described on our journey to Septimont, but the source eluded me at the time.)
(But now... I've found it.)
(The source of that power lies in the Vale of Glory. The closer the Lioness gets to it, the clearer it becomes.)
What is it?
(That girl just now... that was Mya.)
(The closer to the Vale of Glory, the more the normal world and the anomaly <color=RedD>overlap</color>. Whatever lies ahead must be the key to all of this.)
(I need to rethink everything. Septimont, Lupa, Cartethyia, Mya, the Lioness of Glory... all of it.)
(Cartethyia arrived in Septimont alone, not waiting for me or attempting contact. Something must have happened to prevent her.)
(If something cut her off, aside from the Fractsidus, it could've only been the Threnodian, which should be dead.)
(But Septimont holds no faith in the Sentinel, and the Threnodian was never able to establish influence here. Hmm... maybe it's not that simple.)
(The Threnodian controls the minds of Its followers through their faith. Mya is a Ragunnesi, and likely a follower of The Order of the Deep.)
(If the Vale of Glory is an important rite for champions, perhaps Mya went there... and it was there she vanished.)
(Her hysterics, hallucinations, and loss of control in the arena were signs of the Threnodian's corruption.)
(Phrolova once said, "The closer one stands to the <color=RedD>protagonist</color>, the greater their influence over the tale."
(In this Great Agon, Lupa is the <color=RedD>protagonist</color>. Years ago, it was Mya. Now, I'm seeing Mya's image flicker in and out throughout this journey.)
(If the Mya I see is under the Threnodian's control, does that mean securing the <color=RedD>protagonist</color> role is key to Its plan?)
(When I first met Lupa, I sensed what felt like Cartethyia's frequency on her. Later, I also felt the Threnodian's presence.)
(Did they cross paths? If Cartethyia is part of this story, the likelihood is high.)
(Could Lupa have forgotten? Did they meet without realizing it? Or is she lying and already under the Threnodian's influence?)
(Was Phrolova telling the truth by saying Lupa would bring forth the Dark Tide... or is she just another one of its victims?)
There's more at stake than that, Lupa.
I've heard the Septimont saying, "All for glory, fear no death." But sometimes, living takes more courage than dying.
It's okay, don't worry about it.
I'll do what I can.
(It doesn't matter if the <color=RedD>story's end</color> is already written, or yet to unfold...)
(I'll trust my own judgment to guide it where it must go.)
We'll clear it out first, then check if that sword is the source.
Lupa?
The Threnodian's frequency is gone. Well done, Lupa.
I'm fine, Abby. What happened to you earlier?
The knowledge They would later forget...
Not just knowledge...
You've stolen the Sentinel's access too!
Lupa's <color=RedD>role</color> in this story...
Isn't yours!
It's the Threnodian.
Lupa, this is something only you can do.
Read the script. Adapt as required.
I believe in you, warrior.
Am I early? Where's that person Carlotta sent...
Carlotta promised a generous reward once it's done.
An opportunity like this doesn't come round often.
Any backdoors? The Montellis must have designed one.
Alright, let's go.
How much deeper?
More guards are gathering.
He probably found out about the cache from the letter and is attempting to steal it.
He's running blind, getting more desperate as the lockdown escalates. But there's only one way he can go.
And only one thing that can take him there.
Whatever it is, we have no choice but to move forward.
Are you suggesting that people used to live in the vault?
I take it that this "very dark" place you mentioned is the terminus.
We'll be just fine. Ms. Lily, take your time remembering. We'll take it slow.
She could accidentally trigger something dangerous if left alone. That's the last thing we need.
Twenty years in this vault... Sounds like a living hell...
Yeah... The time in the mountains seemed to have slowed down from the outside, away from the Tacet Discords.
The Sentinel must have created a barrier by manipulating the flow of time, keeping those monsters out.
It was a safe haven from all the disturbances caused by the Tacet Discords, where people lived peacefully...
Also... In my dream, I wasn't affected by the slowed time and could freely move in and out of the barrier.
I'm not entirely sure I heard it correctly, but in my dream, the Sentinel mentioned a place called Mt. Firmament.
I had a flashback about seeing their Sentinel... Perhaps that happened on Mt. Firmament...
I can feel an unknown force guiding me, urging me to go there and find something...
Jinhsi said she'll go search for the Sentinel and update me on its whereabouts.
But so far, I have not heard anything from the City Hall yet...
Yeah... The time in the mountains seemed to have slowed down from the outside, away from the Tacet Discords.
The Sentinel must have created a barrier by manipulating the flow of time, keeping those monsters out.
It was a safe haven from all the disturbances caused by the Tacet Discords, where people lived peacefully...
Also... In my dream, I wasn't affected by the slowed time and could freely move in and out of the barrier.
I'm not entirely sure I heard it correctly, but in my dream, the Sentinel mentioned a place called Mt. Firmament.
I had a flashback about seeing their Sentinel... Perhaps that happened on Mt. Firmament...
I can feel an unknown force guiding me, urging me to go there and find something...
Jinhsi said she'll go search for the Sentinel and update me on its whereabouts.
But so far, I have not heard anything from the City Hall yet...
So it can't hurt me...
Someone fought here against those Fractsidus...
The entrance is sealed.
...If I open it, Jinhsi is bound to face you in the ultimate battle.
The ideal outcome would be for her to absorb your power at the critical moment and restore the time flow without any loss of life.
But there's also a chance that she could push herself too far and end up dying from Overclocking.
Though... if I do nothing, you'll use your remaining power to freeze all of Jinzhou in time.
Abby, we've arrived. How do you feel now?
Abby?
(Nothing from my Tacet Mark. Is it sleeping? Or is it...)
(Cartethyia helped with Abby's symptoms before... I could really use her help now.)
(We share the same thread of Imperator's power. I could try using that to sense where she is.)
(What's that creature she's facing? A half-human, half-lion Tacet Discord? Or a special Echo?)
(She was here before.)
(I'll follow this same path and see if I can find someone to talk to.)
(That armor might be an Echo.)
(An Echo... that awaits a true warrior.)
What happened just now? Did they try to ambush you?
Is this tied to the match-fixing you mentioned?
(That's Cartethyia's frequency... but how?)
I'm sorry. Lupa, have you ever met a Ragunnesi girl by the name of Cartethyia?
She's missing. I'm looking for her.
I tracked her to Septimont, and… it seems she confronted Arsinosa.
I see… no leads yet, then.
True. But what's in it for you?
(Arsinosa and the unusual frequency I felt from Lupa are the only leads I've got…)
(Lupa denies ever meeting Cartethyia, but maybe she didn't realize... or she's hiding something.)
(Either way, teaming up with her is my best shot at finding the truth.)
And these are...?
They're just as lively as the baby Gryphon I came across before.
Okay, but how?
So… this is Septimont.
No response.
Can you tell where these traces lead, Abby?
That way… That's the archipelago across from Ragunna.
The traces are still fresh. We should follow them to find Cartethyia before they fade.
Yes. With the Threnodian's will gone, she wouldn't lose contact unless something's wrong. Really wrong.
But before we go…
I want to learn more about the archipelago before I set off to find her.
What kind of differences?
Sounds like the perfect time to visit Septimont. Thank you, Cantarella.
No, I'm looking for someone.
(After Cartethyia and Fleurdelys's frequencies merged, she got her body back. If she passed through here, people would've seen her.)
Can you tell me more about this Guardian Echo?
(But... why would Cartethyia take on Septimont's Guardian Echo?)
(Its frequency is similar to the Echoes in Ragunna. Could it have originated from Imperator's power as well?)
(But it's unstable, as if something else is interfering... Strange, it's just like Abby said.)
(I can feel its source close to the city, but can't pin down where exactly.)
And you are...?
How did you know my name?
What was that smirk about?
Are you planning to muddy the waters?
(Cartethyia... standing on the water?)
(That abnormal sensation came over me again when my sword struck the contender's Echo. )
(All the Echoes in Septimont seem to exhibit unstable power. Something is definitely off.)
(But it seems like no one else has noticed.)
I've been meaning to ask, is there a body of water in the city? I think Cartethyia might've passed through there.
Thanks. And…
Those people just now… they talked about match-fixing. Don't you want to challenge that?
This is the place. I'll look for Cartethyia's traces here.
Through... the connection we share.
Who are they...?
Just the two of us? Is it two-on-two?
What's Heartpour?
Restored footage?
Do you think we'll face her in this Agon?
You knew this was coming?
(Lupa... It was the Threnodian and Dark Tide's frequencies I sensed in her weapon, if only for a brief moment.)
(She thought it was just the Silvas' dirty trick and didn't notice its connection with the Threnodian.)
(Phrolova said her associate can <color=RedD>weave fate</color> and <color=RedD>stories</color>. If that's true, it means his Forte can influence how things play out.)
(But it's unlikely he can freely bend the fabric of reality. The Fractsidus would've overrun Solaris long ago if he could. Either Septimont exists outside reality, or his ability has limits.)
(If this isn't the real world, I could take more drastic actions. But if it is, killing Lupa would be an act I could never take back.)
(The best approach is to stick to my original plan. Wait for Arsinosa to appear in the final. That's my lead to Cartethyia.)
(I also sensed Cartethyia's frequency on Lupa, too. If she's here, she's making moves, just like I am.)
(In the meantime, I need to figure out that Overseer's limits. The longer this lasts, the more anomalies are bound to show.)
Buling? Why are you here?
How much do you know about it? This is our second time meeting, Buling. That can't be a coincidence.
Buling? Ah, she's gone again.
(I remember Lupa mentioning a Gladiator named Mya. She disappeared years ago during a Great Agon.)
(Phrolova said the <color=RedD>story</color> only lasts for three days and ends during the final. The Great Agon is at the heart of the story after all.)
(The bell on this <te href=850158>mirror</te> is jingling... The closer I move in that direction, the louder it gets. I'd better check it out.)
(For an instant, I felt it. The Threnodian's frequency again.)
Lupa, can you tell me more about Mya?
Just curious. I overheard some people talking about her earlier.
(So that's the Guardian Echo of Septimont...)
(...But there's something different about this Arsinosa compared to the one I saw confronting Cartethyia.)
Lupa...?
Lupa, how did we win?
I see...
(The Bloody Agon I experienced definitely wasn't a dream. It was like the real world <color=RedD>overlapped</color> with the anomalous one.)
(I kept seeing Lupa and Mya <color=RedD>overlap</color> with each other as well.)
(That fleeting frequency... It was similar to the Threnodian's. Could it really be linked to the Dark Tide Lupa will bring forth in Septimont, as Phrolova said?)
Lupa, can I see your spear?
(Hmm, I can't feel that abnormal frequency anymore...)
Lupa, does Domina Julia believe in the Sentinel?
...I see.
Go ahead.
I'll ask again, Phrolova. What are you doing here in Septimont, and why give me this?
So you're not the only Overseer active in Rinascita.
Scar, your nameless ally, and you... The Fractsidus seems to revel in the company of those with twisted obsessions.
(I've already crossed paths with him since arriving in Rinascita…?)
What you're proposing I do... Tell me, what reason do I have to trust a word you're saying, Phrolova?
I'd say your first wish has already been fulfilled.
Cartethyia...
Huh? Are you drinking...?
...Sure, wheat juice.
Lupa, you've had enough wheat juice. Sit. Take a break for a moment.
Have you ever thought about living a different life, Lupa?
(What's this? The Plushies brought back a recipe for Baton Bread?)
Yeah. I see a new member in the village. An oven Echo?
I happen to have a bread recipe. Can I ask Old Bake to make me some?
The graph here says, “The reboot of the Refinery Tower takes a certain amount of time. First, secure a sufficient power supply during the rebooting...”
Yes. All ready and set. What about your part?
What are they talking about? Are they Lei'li and Modee?
I should go and ask them what happened to the Mine.
It felt as if I was seeing through the eyes of a Tacet Discord. I think... I saw what was happening in the Norfall Barrens.
According to Baizhi, the leaf showed residual fluctuations of Tacet Discords.
Those fluctuations pointed to a certain set of coordinates.
(In the south...)
You'd better...
Let her go!
I will not leave anyone behind!
("Tune in" to my memories... Does that mean I'm supposed to see the past from another perspective?)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Someone used their Resonance ability to help me relive this moment, but this seems... unexpected.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The Sentinel—Imperator's voice guided me to you.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Well, Imperator called you "the Lost," and Primus Fenrico claims you are the Resonator for Leviathan, the Threnodian.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: But you really don't have any idea?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: They spoke of being ensnared in an inverted tower suspended in the sky.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Abby has a nose for these sorts of things. We came across Threnodian powers before in Jinzhou.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: So it's safe to say Abby can be trusted.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: If we're making assumptions, let's err on the optimistic side first.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Given your current situation, it is possible you were affected by the Threnodian's powers during your battle alongside Imperator.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: That could be the reason behind losing all your power, too.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I want to know what's going on with Imperator in that tower. And I want to speak with you in person.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: How can I reach your Sonoro Sphere?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: You said no one can get in. Has anyone tried?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Any distinguishing features?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I'll look for a way to find this person you mentioned.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: No, it was really helpful.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (The Fisalia family? The one that's likely worshipping the Threnodian?)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Why does she want to talk with me?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Tell her I accept the invitation, but at a later time.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (The way she describes the head of Fisalia... reminds me of how Cartethyia spoke about the wandering figure by the barrier.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (Can't rule out the possibility that they're the same person. Looks like I'll need to pay her a visit.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (Cantarella said that only those who resonate with Tyrvine are granted glimpses of its knowledge...)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Don't jump to conclusions just yet. That doesn't prove you're the Threnodian's Resonator, nor that you aid Its cause.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Right now, you're the sole survivor of whatever happened that day. You really don't remember anything?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: If we do nothing, we'll never get any answers.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Instead of speculating, we should use the clues we've gathered so far.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: There might still be a path forward.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Carlotta once helped me interpret some stained glass images. The story it told was very similar to what you've described.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Only more biased towards the Order. It mentioned a maiden who would emerge as the Sentinel's Resonator and later save Rinascita.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Do you think that's also a lie?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Then... what about Cartethyia?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (An inverted ruin suspended in the sky... her and Cartethyia's descriptions bear many similarities.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (If Tyrvine is the key to entering the Sonoro Sphere, it stands to reason that Imperator may have played a role in the Sphere's creation.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (If I can confirm that Tyrvine was truly left by Imperator, at least it would prove that neither are lying.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (Cantarella said she was Overclocking when she tried resonating with Tyrvine.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (That's exactly what happened with Jinhsi. Could Imperator be trying to restore Their power through a Second Awakening?)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (If that's the case... my permission should cause a reaction. That's one way to confirm what they're saying.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Well, I have some information and theories of my own. Care to hear them?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I'm guessing putting Tyrvine on the pedestal has something to do with it.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The power to split and manipulate space...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: If Imperator set this in motion, then Their original plan has already failed.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Cartethyia, that means your guidance holds the key now.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I believe in you.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Is there anything else you remember?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: But I didn't see the Dark Tide.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Shh... later.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Someone needs a quiet moment.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Feeling better?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The answer might lie in the other frequencies that got mixed in.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The answer might lie in the other frequencies that got mixed in.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: You are a Resonator born of the merge between the Sentinel and Threnodian. What if you used their abilities to split it yourself?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The answer might lie behind the third sealed passageway.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: (Cartethyia is starting to make sense of things, but she'll need time to get her head straight.)
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Same as before? We'll keep watch tonight and continue our journey tomorrow.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I heard your request...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: But I prefer to handle this my way.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Imperator? I thought you...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: With unrestricted access, you were going to...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: So, what did you learn?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: What role does Cartethyia play in this?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: What made you decide to leave Tyrvine with the Fisalias?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: But that wasn't your original plan.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I don't know if the end justifies the means.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: ...I can't speak for what I wasn't a part of.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: You did what you could do in that moment. Anyone still standing because of it doesn't get to judge.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Call her Cartethyia, that's what she wanted.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: How do you intend to bring her back?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The only question now is how we reach Cartethyia and Imperator's Sonoro Sphere.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: So you're the one that Cartethyia sensed wandering outside the Sonoro Sphere?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Morning Cartethyia.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Mm-hmm. Let's do it.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: This tea... Is it from Jinzhou?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Before we get into that, there's something I want to know.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Why haven't the Threnodian and the Sentinel ever shown themselves?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Let me ask you this one more time. Should I call you Cartethyia or Fleurdelys?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Very well, Cartethyia.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: We might encounter something similar at the next two pedestals.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Keep an eye on this for me, will you?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I understand...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: She needs time.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: What's this?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Is that the only one?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Before I forget, Imperator left a message.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: "From this day forth. You are free."
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Will you tell the others?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I'll take my leave then.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: If Carlotta asked you to find me, has something happened?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Recreate the frequency?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Were you singing just then?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: No... I'm sure they can feel what's in your heart.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I'm not just saying that to comfort you. I believe it's possible.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I once saw two friends, one who was already dead, reconcile with each other from within a Sonoro Sphere.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: When frequencies are concentrated in one place, it's possible that their Reverberations could still carry traces of human will.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: They might still be able to hear and feel.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Your voice, your goodbyes, your feelings... could have reached them.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: After all, these frequencies are strong enough to manifest in the real world.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: They haven't made a move against us. They must still remember you, which means they can probably hear you, too.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: You can ease up a little. As long as their obsession holds and the Sonoro Sphere stays intact, they won't vanish.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Cartethyia?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Hey, since we know you don't carry any blame, should I call you Cartethyia or Fleurdelys?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I'll handle this.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: …Mayo? Is that you?
What is this place?
So let's avoid it. Which way is faster?
The right one seems closer and safer.
There's an inscription on the altar.
"I am no longer Them. I will lead the people forward."
Imperator's statue is gone… Is this Fenrico's ambition?
The next line reads...
"It reminds us that the Sentinel walks with us, illuminating our path with wisdom."
The Sentinel… Could this lantern be the very one Napoli the Second used? The one imbued with the Sentinel's power that allowed him to repel the Dark Tide?
This is far from undisturbed.
(She's trying to use her Forte again… and she looks better now. Her injuries and Resonance ability are recovering.)
(Our alliance hinges on her weakened state. I'm not sure how long it'll take for her to fully recover… but when she does, this fragile pact could end in an instant.)
Phrolova.
Things are getting worse in the Lost Beyond?
The Sonoro Sphere you created seems to be tied to your life.
(Lighting the Lumenstones also stirs Phantoms of the past.)
The Bloody Agon is no more. Anyone who disgraces the arena's honor, noble or not, will face justice.
By the request of apprentice Gladiator Verbia, I challenge you to a duel.
By the request of apprentice Gladiator Verbia, I challenge you to a duel.
The Dark Tide didn't take her. She held on until I arrived.
...The Dark Tide didn't take her. She held on until I arrived.
You only played noble because you weren't the one in the arena.
We already searched the ship from top to bottom. There wasn't anything that looked like a star.
I'm here with you. Don't worry.
Don't worry, Luca, it's no trouble at all. You also have something important to do—rest and recover.
And just like you said, the star watches over everyone. We're doing it for everybody.
It's like the mist has been waiting for us.
It's… split in half?
Mmm?
Look, there's something there…
Is that a letter? Does it mean anything to you?
Makes sense. But what's got him heading to Egla Town at midnight?
That's quite the legend you've built yourself
(He sounds sincere. Can I really trust him?)
(Regardless, agreeing to his deal to get ahold of the other key is in my best interest. Even if it's a trap, we can handle it. In that case...)
Deal. Hand over the key.
Deal. Hand over the key.
We managed to slip away.
Amidst all your theatrics, you always remain true to your code.
Agreed. We need to gather intel without being spotted. Let's find a way to sneak past.
How do I meet with one of them?
The last person that those robots likely saw before they lost control was Maja's mother.
I think we can talk to her under the guise of a friendly house visit.
But aren't you going to tell me I should report this to the Academy to handle?
(Not sure who here knows what Yngvar did. Best not to mention his name. Don't want to stir things up.)
We rescued this Soliskin from a Void Storm. We were told it needed to return here for the Rite of Return.
(I forgot about that. Without a solid reason, he might start to suspect something.)
You were quick on your feet earlier. You've already got this.
With that in mind, you can relax a bit now, right?
Then it's settled.
I'll join you. Let's dance with Buddy. What do you say?
Our real battle starts tomorrow.
Tonight, let's relax a little, Mornye.
We can drop the act now, right? Or do you think we should continue?
This Void Storm might not have just affected the woods here, but the workings of Solisylum itself.
I think that's why I've been seeing visions of this member of the Six.
What's the matter, Mornye？
Hi, are you Astrid, Maja's mom?
Professor Mornye, could you explain how Maja's robot caused the damage?
(Her demeanor changed just now. She's hiding something. Maybe more pressure.)
We need to understand the full scope of the robot's malfunction. We understand that this morning, it was you who helped Maja activate the robots and enable auto-run.
We need to ensure the safety of those here first.
Questions can wait. Let's go.
(The voice from earlier said it would share the last of the Heliogyre's strength so it wouldn't fade. It must be that Soliskin. Did it eventually vanish here?)
No. The trail ends here. But I saw other things in that memory.
In that memory, I heard a voice say he would force the Heliogyre to a standstill.
(When I handled the Voidworm, a voice told me to protect the sun. Whoever she is, I don't think she intends harm. She offered to help. For now, I'll do what she suggested.)
(And one thing is certain. The one who knows me by my "Arbiter" title needs my help.)
(Hm. Yet another problem tied to the Research Institute. We shouldn't reveal our plans to them just yet.)
I need to find out who he is, Mornye. Do you know any Roya who are familiar with the tribe's inner workings?
Something wrong?
We did promise to witness it together, didn't we?
I... saw someone's memory.
Can you trace where it came from, Abby?
Good. Let's go check it out.
(But he could harbor animosity toward the current Heliodic Six. Yngvar is still our lead to find them.)
(Things are getting more complicated. A direct approach with Yngvar might not be a good idea.)
Hmm. Where did Mornye go? I'll send her a <ano=WavesLine>message</ano>.
A light blue ring?
Can you describe what you saw in more detail?
(It sounds like the girl Mornye saw is the same one I encountered when I first got to Lahai-Roi.)
(She's able to go up against the Threnodian herself, and appear in the Reactor Drive. She's connected with the Exostrider and the Threnodian in ways we still don't fully understand.)
(Figuring out who she is and what happened to her will be the next big task.)
Heh, a photo under the new Helios? Sounds like it'll make a good souvenir.
To new beginnings.
You mentioned before that someone single-handedly wiped out the upper ranks of the Black Alley. Any idea who it was?
A lot of people were targeted, but in the end, they only sent you to handle it?
The biggest issue I find is...
By tradition, shouldn't you have revealed your true identity at the end?
It's hard to imagine that name suiting you now.
Do you think this "Talos" is the real deal then?
Colleen, if you can't trust my friend, then I can't offer you my help.
Trust goes both ways. You can't ask for help with one hand while holding a knife with the other.
Your instincts came from a good place. A fist might not be as sharp as a blade, but it can still break through deeply rooted problems.
And up close, it's gentle enough to protect what's important.
So don't let today bother you.
Someone's making moves. But they just seem to be petty thieves.
And it's the people of a civilization who decide what they demand.
I want you to have this, Pheobe.
Imperator entrusted me with this relic. Within it is Rinascita's past, and... its future.
The future... is already in your hands.
Sign me up.
How's everything going?
Everyone in the Black Shores has made it their mission to stand against the Lament. Our goals are the same, and we've been walking the same path from the start.
I know you're not the type who likes being tied down by titles. But just remember this... You're not fighting it alone. So...
This flower marks you as one of us. Whether you join us or not, if you ever run into trouble out there, the Black Shores will have your back.
Don't worry about her. Nothing's stopping that one. No storm, and no raging sea.
You're welcome. By the way, Phoebe, have you seen Galbrena?
Yes, their ship should have docked by now.
He seems intimidating, but... I think he's alright. He did join the fight to protect Rinascita, after all.
So… till we meet again.
You once said the Harbinger had to be corrupted by the Dark Tide. But I still don't show any signs of corruption...
We made a call when it mattered. I asked you to absorb the Dark Tide. We couldn't have known what it would become. You didn't do anything wrong.
...From what I know, Leviathan manifests Its kingdom by flooding Rinascita with the Dark Tide. That's how it turns the real world into the Journeying Paradise.
But we've already secured Ragunna and Septimont. The Fabricatorium and the hunting grounds are both stabilized... So why is this Paradise still there?
I heard them. All those voices in the Dark Tide. And before I touched the gate... I felt it. Fear. Sorrow. Like it was pouring out of the people.
Okay... But how are you feeling with "that thing" in you? We've wrapped everything up here. Best we get to the Black Shores soon.
Abby, time to dig in!
Miss Margherita, one extra large Forte-astic Fizz & Freeze gelato, please. Thank you.
Now that's something I really look forward to.
Oh, that... don't worry, money's not an issue.
Augusta made sure we're covered. All our food and lodging here in Septimont is billed straight to the Ephor's Palace.
But...
I think the owner might've overdone it a little...
Abby, what do you think Imperator wants me to find? If Cartethyia's right, the last clues should be somewhere on the <te href=851028>Sanguis Plateaus</te>.
We're so close to the finish line, just one step away. But our pace has slowed...
A messenger came this morning. Augusta is dealing with an emergency, so our meeting's postponed... looks like it'll be a while.
An unexpected change. It's throwing everything off course... I don't like the feel of it.
And why me for this hunt? If it's about the Great Agon... the messenger said none of the other Gladiators were invited.
You think so?
I've only met her a few times, but I doubt she acts on such simple impulses.
And as the Ephor, is she really in a position to do whatever she wants?
Finding that Gondola might be a good start.
(Let's hope she stays in this delusion until she makes it back to the city.)
It's a real apple...
I'm starting to understand why Carlotta calls you <ano=Sunlit>"Topaz"</ano>.
That's strange, she was just there a moment ago.
Why don't we split up and look for her at the harbor?
Is this Ciaccona's sheet music? She was here.
The vision I just saw seems to have been recorded from this...
Ciaccona mentioned she always writes down her melodies on manuscript paper. Could this be...?
The Golden Anthem...
But I haven't even heard you sing yet.
This song... it's the one she performed last time.
The melody is the same, but why does it sound so sad this time?
The will of the people is strong. This mastermind is so sure that the lies about the Sentinel will go undiscovered, and that no one will easily believe the truth.
The truth exists to be uncovered. All we need is to plant the seeds of doubt in the people's hearts and let them take root and grow.
—a friend of mine.
The Poets Society was criticized for glorifying the knights over the common folk during one of their hymn performances.
Their assumptions were challenged, so they traveled to the knight's encampment at Whisperwind Haven to verify the truth for themselves.
I just walked into a study and ran into a strange little girl. She seemed quite taken with the Poets Society's work.
She mentioned a name—Ciaccona Toccata
Really, I ended up nowhere in particular.
I'll go check it out. Knowing Chisa, she usually doesn't act without purpose.
(I don't think I was imagining it... that voice.)
Let's take care of important matters first. You'll eat well soon, I promise.
At least now we know it's called the "Civilization Capsule." It probably holds key legacy data left behind by the Sentinel.
Maybe because they fused together at some point...
I'm fine. What I saw, though…
(What were those Tacet Discords whispering about? Gates of Paradise... and the word "Harbinger." Could they mean the "Harbinger of the Dark Tide"?)
(The only "Harbinger of the Dark Tide" is Fenrico. But he should still be sealing the Dark Tide at the Order's Fabricatorium...)
(That figure I saw... and Cartethyia's murder...)
(I came back to find her, so I should look for her first. She might know what's at the root of all this.)
You're getting ready to leave?
Did you realize you were in a Sonoro at the start?
It's from the Black Shores... Let's find a quieter spot.
These coordinates bring us closer to Ragunna.
The more people involved, the more attention we might draw from the Order. Their eyes are everywhere, including on our friends.
Besides, we've seen before how the Rinascitans are more vulnerable to the Threnodian's power. If they get involved, it could put them in danger.
I'm not here to play games. Cut to the chase, what are you after?
Ah, so you and the Fractsidus can't agree on how to split the Threnodian's residual energy.
Ha, I trust what I see. There is no need for me to ally with you.
You couldn't reach me? Must've been due to the chaotic frequencies at the seabed. Relax, I'm fine.
You were right. The Threnodian's power at the seabed came from Mya.
It crystallized within a Gem, luring Fenrico to open a rift for the Dark Tide to seep through. It tried to escape back into the Tide to merge with the remaining Threnodian power to grow stronger.
Sure. I'm heading to Septimont to follow the clues Fenrico left behind.
Fenrico, stop! The Threnodian is using you! You are the Harbinger of the Dark Tide!
Your plan won't work.
You've already tried everything! Can't you see? The path you're on only leads here, nowhere beyond.
What meaning is there in chasing something that's neither realistic nor attainable?
Those fading Reverberations did not, and cannot, return to life. No matter how long you hold onto their frequencies, the result will be the same.
The villagers are dead. What you've created are Tacet Discords. If you continue turning the entire town into that... you're not creating a paradise. You're creating a hell.
It's what they forced on you. And the Triss you saw? Fleeting, unstable, an illusion. And yet, you still believe everything they say can become real?
You've gone too far.
You never understood the true value of human life.
(Fenrico spoke of a... Paradise. What does it mean?)
(And "save Rinascita's souls"... Was he pointing to the source of the anomaly in Rinascita?)
We were after the Gem to find the source of the anomaly in Rinascita.
Now we have a better lead—the Journeying Paradise.
That might also explain why the Fractsidus took the Gem and what they're really after.
We'll head to Septimont to uncover the truth.
Maybe it's time we spoke with the Ephor.
Abby, Echo interpretation service, please.
(I knew it. They're all in on it... Crooks stealing from other crooks. This auction was just one big setup from the start.)
(I knew it. They're all in on it... Crooks stealing from other crooks. This auction was just one big setup from the start.)
(I knew it. They're all in on it... Crooks stealing from other crooks. This auction was just one big setup from the start.)
(I knew it. They're all in on it... Crooks stealing from other crooks. This auction was just one big setup from the start.)
(But the tale of Augusta's trial only mentions an old hunter. Who's this second person?)
lines.quest_name: Dream of A Rusted Sword: Part I

lines.text: (Someone left out of the story...)
lines.quest_name: Dream of A Rusted Sword: Part I

lines.text: Not really. A lot of it came from... the person that was with me at the time. She was one of the founders of this place.
Strange. The melody of this place has gone back to normal. The Echoes shouldn't be behaving like this.
This is the control hub of the entire Fabricatorium, overseeing all of its areas. Each region has a similar device that reacts to every change.
Someone once told me that everything is made of frequencies, melodies can bend the rhythm of existence, and by orchestrating their essence, we reshape their very nature.
The key to Fabricatorium's operations is a melody. Keep this melody, make sure it plays well, and the Fabricatorium will remain stable.
If you want to make a change here, you'll have to adjust the melody. No way around it.
I do.
(I knew it. They're all in on it... Crooks stealing from other crooks. This auction was just one big setup from the start.)
(I knew it. They're all in on it... Crooks stealing from other crooks. This auction was just one big setup from the start.)
Follow your heart. Do what's right.
(Looks like Phoebe is swamped at the moment. Better give her a hand before she gets overwhelmed.)
Shouldering every responsibility can sometimes be irresponsible in itself.
Leave the tasks in Septimont to me. I've gotten pretty familiar with the area.
Come to think of it, if Brant had put you on the right ship, you wouldn't have ended up in Mournfell Canyon... So really, it is Brant's fault.
...Now will you tell me what's really going on with the fan club?
Have you thought about being the Peaks of Prestige fan club's advisor, Professor?
I've seen something like this before. Whether it's the Tethys System's simulations or so-called fated "prophecies," human will can defy predicted outcomes to create new futures.
Maybe that's what the "unknown variable" in your model really represents.
By the way, Professor Festa, you don't really seem to care too much about disbanding the Peaks of Prestige fan club, do you?
Understood. I'll head over right away.
(Alright. Time to contact the Spacetrek Collective to have them arrest the Outcasts.
We wouldn't want my lack of artistic expertise to give the Shanyun Art Exchange a chance to turn the hearing against us.
Don't worry, I've got your back. Just remember, you're the only one who can lay out the truth—the whole truth— for everyone to see.
Madam, allow us to present our account of the events.
This all really began three days ago.
I was entrusted to investigate a certain "Arcane Artist" in the Tiger's Maw.
Nothing here. That's odd... Did I just imagine that?
When I asked him about the incident, he put all the blame on Mingyan, saying she had ulterior motives.
The Exiles we questioned during the day did the same, trying to divert attention and cover up their own involvement at the scene in the first place.
These distractions led us to concentrate solely on Mingyan, ignoring other possible angles.
This evidence clearly shows that these paintings are no ordinary copies, but your way of making a profit.
While I have yet to uncover my past and who I am, every moment with you is a new and special memory.
Thank you for your help, Zhezhi. This experience has helped me understand you and the world a little better.
When I asked him about the incident, he put all the blame on Mingyan, saying she had ulterior motives.
The Exiles we questioned during the day did the same, trying to divert attention and cover up their own involvement at the scene in the first place.
These distractions led us to concentrate solely on Mingyan, ignoring other possible angles.
(Once Zhezhi has the paintbrush in her hand, she begins to speak calmly.)
(She'll be fine making the statement on her own now.)
(Once Zhezhi has the paintbrush in her hand, she begins to speak calmly.)
(She'll be fine making the statement on her own now.)
It says Augusta has taken care of everything on her end. She's ready to meet us at the Ephor's Palace as agreed.
It's most likely something to do with the <te href=750001>hunt</te>.
We shouldn't keep the Ephor waiting... Time to go, Abby.
That's... a storm...
Which means it's about time to meet up with Augusta.
Tempest Cliffs. I remember that's where she told us to go.
This... should be the right way...
I don't understand. Given Augusta's prestige, the Senate has no real advantage in seizing power. What's their angle?
Is that lady over there... looking at me?
Better go and greet her.
Meaning we need to hunt and kill the beast near the Earthrender... Is that how we stop the Dark Tide from spreading?
A Consultant active in Rinascita?
So it is her...
Why don't you let me handle contacting the Black Shores, Augusta?
We... talk from time to time. It'll help make things smoother for us.
Is that...?
Abby!
In that case… I could say the same to you.
As the prophecy goes, "The Hero of Heroes wielding extraordinary power, rising from the dark tides." As I see it, you, Augusta, fit that image well.
What really happened to you in the Dark Tide? I thought you might've...
I want to ask you something.
Why did you ask me to join the hunt?
Imperator...
The Black Shores' records on the Sentinel may give us more clues.
More importantly, I need their data on the Dark Tide.
The Earthrender's holding back the Dark Tide for now, but we're not in the clear yet. Those sudden outbreaks and the way it's fixated on you… it's not just a coincidence.
I'll contact them as soon as we're back.
There's a friend of mine who… well, she knows a lot about Imperator. She might be able to help solve this mystery.
More importantly... her powers are unique. The Earthrender's holding back the Dark Tide for now, but those sudden outbreaks and the way it's fixated on you… it's not just a coincidence.
If anyone can put an end to the Dark Tide's threat in Septimont, it's her.
You two share a lot in common, actually. I think you'd get along.
I'll reach out to her once we're back. She wasn't able to travel before due to some unavoidable reasons, but now, I think the timing's right.
Augusta...
So Murmurstown... I mean, Fabianum, is your hometown?
Or maybe... it meant to show us something more.
Me? Honestly, the thought never crossed my mind.
From what I've seen, being a true ruler isn't about having immense strength, whether that's a powerful Resonance ability or sheer might.
I've met people from Huanglong to Rinascita who live with real integrity. Their skills and backgrounds were all different, and... none of them were perfect.
But what defines them isn't strength or anything that can be measured. It's the spirit they were born with. Resilient. Unyielding. That can't be stolen.
Believe me, Augusta, I see that same spirit in you.
A Dark Tide occurrence sixteen years ago... Its frequency must have been more stable than it is now.
Maybe this is the truth the Gladiator's spirit is trying to share...
Perhaps this could work.
You're suggesting... we let the Sovereign absorb the Anchor Stone? If it works, it could suppress it from the inside.
...Fleet Snowfluff?
So you weren't just a Synchronist. You had a whole other life here.
You saved Lahai-Roi, Aemeath. But you paid for it with your future, and no one even knows...
That place above the sword?
(My frequency loss… It's getting worse!)
I'm… okay. After that mech flew off, I felt less of my frequency bleeding out.
(That mech's frequency felt like the presence from the rift back in Honami City.)
Aemeath, that mech only left after it saw you. After you clashed with it.
That's twice you've saved me. Each time I'm left with more questions than answers. They say you're the Exostrider's Synchronist who's been missing for over ten years.
And your hand back there...
So you Overclocked yourself to try to resonate with it?
"Not long ago"... You mean when the Reactor Drive fell?
Then it was you. You're the one who saved Mornye when she was dying.
(A junior of mine? So I really did know Mornye in the past...)
I can tell you're leaving a few things out, Aemeath.
Still, you helped me and a lot of people in Lahai-Roi. That's enough for me to trust you. What do you need me to do?
I'll do it.
I'm fine. What about you?
You know him?
You know how to modify bikes?
Was it the Lament that changed it into frostlands?
I understand the Etheric Sea part, but the Stridergate…?
Debris?
Luuk, Nivora. You alright?
The collapse was an accident. We're all okay, so don't blame yourself.
But the way we came in is completely blocked.
What about waiting inside the station? It's freezing out here, and another collapse is possible. It's safer to wait for the rescue team indoors.
What do you mean?
(Was that... me in that recording?)
(That had to have been the station's final moments, when the "<te href=750035>Etheric Sea</te> Phenomenon" suddenly spread and engulfed all of Solaris, like Luuk said.)
(But those droplets in the sky… that was Retroact Rain. So that might've been an image of an event long ago, not part of the Etheric Sea Phenomenon itself.)
(Did I really fall from the Etheric Sea? Or what lay beyond it? Junxi did say the droplets could act as some kind of gateway.)
(Back then, could I have...)
Aemeath?
(...Very few people know about that.)
Be direct with me, Luuk.
I know you knew me before. But I acted mostly as a Bloom Bearer in the past.
You and I, what exactly are we to each other?
Alright. I'll see you back at the infirmary.
Luuk, when you said I'm "not the same," what did you mean?
Aemeath?! I've been looking for you.
I never got the chance to thank you.
The day the Eye of the Threnodian appeared, you drove it back and saved me.
(She doesn't sound like she's lying. Then how...)
What's so funny?
Aemeath?
President Lucilla, what do you know about her?
Nivora!
(Hm? That must've been Lucilla.)
(Aemeath definitely glossed over some things.)
(Like what happened during our previous encounter, what she's done since becoming a digital ghost, and the real reason why Sigillum stole her power.)
(After years drifting like this, to still hold onto that spirit... Her will must be strong. Prying answers from her won't be easy.)
(I'll bide my time. The right moment will come.)
Mornye?
…You were monitoring my comms with Mornye.
"We"?
President Lucilla, you're asking a lot of questions. And I don't appreciate being interrogated over a tapped line.
…Why is the Spacetrek Collective pushing this so hard?
The third wish... Let's see what's written this time.
(He might need some time alone to process what just happened...)
Every time the Lament struck, you moved toward Neon Tower, against the tide of fleeing citizens, knowing full well it was the most dangerous place to be.
You knew you couldn't save those who were already "gone," and still, you reached out. To the mother and daughter. To the injured young man. To me, when I first fell into this city.
You knew that in this endless loop, saving someone didn't change a thing. But still, not once did you ignore a cry for help. So...
Which means there's a chance to break it completely. I'm willing to put my faith in you. Let's seize that chance together.
Because we're partners. Isn't it only natural for me to trust you?
What happened?
(…I think I heard something.)
Alright. I'll call if I get anything.
Who took it?
Were you following me?
To its owner… Are you that "Lady Flora"?
(If Camellya is Lady Flora, then we were supposed to be close, but neither of us remembers…)
(The fact that I used to be the leader... This should only be known to the Shorekeeper.)
(Maybe I shouldn't mention it to Camellya until I'm sure she's not a threat.)
That fleeing Stellar Matrix might affect the Necrostar’s functioning. Worse, it could threaten the Black Shores.
The info in that matrix is related to you. With you by my side, I should be able to locate and capture it faster.
We need to solve this accident as soon as possible, and you promised to help if I played the game with you.
What else do you know about Lady Flora? You said a small accident happened during your investigation...
It doesn't sound like you're talking about your past self.
Sure. Whoever finds it first will go meet up with the other.
This Blake Bloom is…
What exactly is this bracelet…?
…I wanted to help you up.
You're on the edge of Overclocking. And you were in a medical pod before... Does this happen often?
It's very dangerous. You know the risks, right?
(Around the same time? The two cases might be connected...)
Augusta, do you remember Iuno?
Yes, she was often by your side. The one with the bow like the moon. She joined our hunt for the Sovereign...
This bracelet belongs to Iuno.
How can this be? You spent the most time with her.
Iuno.
So you've known this all along...
Iuno once said she glimpsed the end, where she would be the last one standing. But now, this end never came... and Iuno is dead.
Is this the cost of changing destiny? Did she know she'd disappear, but accepted it anyway?
Why am I special? Why has everyone forgotten everything about Iuno except me?
But if I'm the exception, if I'm the only one who still remembers... does that mean there's something I can do about it?
She didn't even get to finish her sentence. What was she thinking? Why did she do it... If this is the end, maybe I'll never know.
I don't care if Iuno foresaw her fate and chose this path for herself.
All I know is I won't let it end like this. Not with her fading into oblivion.
Why did Iuno choose this? Did she truly believe this was the better ending?
I'm the one she entrusted with her bracelet. The exception. So it's on me to find the answer.
Like Augusta said, everything that exists leaves a trace. Since she's a Priestess, I'm sure to find traces of her in Tetragon Temple.
A lead could be the breakthrough I need.
Looks like the oration they mentioned.
Huh… I thought for sure it'd be Iuno at an event like this.
Eh, well. Guess I'll catch her later.
Prophecy of the end, and how we managed to lure the Sovereign out... The order and the story are right, but something's missing.
You speak of me and Augusta, but what of Iuno?
Iuno is a Priestess, just like you.
More than that, she wielded the Moon Bow and fought alongside me and Augusta in the hunt. We defeated the Sovereign together... Don't you all remember?
No, this can't be.
I don't know what a Priestess is supposed to be, but I do know what Iuno is like. And I'm certain... she's a Priestess.
You mentioned cutting through the Dark Tide's miasma. Tell me, if Iuno wasn't there, then who shot the Moon Arrow?
(Didn't you tell me we would meet at the very end? Then why can't I find you...)
Anchor, manifest, whatever you want to call it. All I know is Iuno's not gone because of me. So why shouldn't I be able to bring her back?
If I can tangle the causal chain, why can't I create a new possibility?
(Their accounts are all true, except when it comes to Iuno. How they react to her name is completely wrong.)
(Everything she did, they attribute to me. They're not just forgetting. It's as if Iuno never existed to them at all.)
(Could this be possible? This distortion in perception? Is it a remnant of the Sovereign's power? Or is this the work of the Fractsidus again?)
If there were any doubt, now... Now I'm sure.
I'm not rejecting the ending Iuno wrote. I just want to fix the regret she left behind.
This isn't the ending I'm happy with, that's all.
An unnecessary death traded for an unforetold victory... She gave everything for Septimont only to be forgotten.
That's a fate no one should suffer. Not her, not anyone.
We've only just begun our story. The future I want... is one where she's there with all of us.
Forgetting may be easier than remembering. But let that be the choice of someone alive, not one who's already gone.
Right now I... don't want to lose anything I still have.

lines.text: What if the footage was tampered with? Her presence could've been erased.
What was that device you had when Hyvatia went berserk?
Highly unlikely.
What's your real purpose?
(Those were… my memories? But the last one…)
...No, I'm fine. So, this is a <te href=700006>Rabelle Cassette</te>?
(Could it have been me who left it behind? But if I deleted it myself… why leave any trace?)
Professor Mornye, about my frequency...
Me? But... I'm just a freshman.
I'd be honored to witness the rise of Lahai-Roi's new sun.
What's the situation?
Then we need to find a way to get out there.
Professor Mornye, I need to borrow an Institute vehicle.
The same way I got in!
You like racing that much?
But you're already a student here. You have what it takes to chase your dreams too.
Well then, pleased to meet you... Miss Lynae?
Is it really so important to you?
And you want to be one of them.
...A "student" wouldn't talk like that.
Even if we solve this, do you think you'll be able to look at yourself in the mirror afterward?
That being said, I do know what matters most now.
I stood up to Warren for you because I chose to trust you. And I'll own that choice.
Let's do this. Together.
But why would the Academy allow something this dangerous to circulate freely?
Back to those waveforms… If one is Hyvatia's, then the other two are likely the targets for the next experiment.
That means... you're coming back to the Academy.
(Lynae... did she really leave?)
(Either way, stopping this from getting worse is all that matters now. She said the Fractsidus wouldn't count on only one device.)
(If they can control the lure remotely, then whoever's guiding the Voidworm probably isn't even on campus.)
(It's unrealistic to try to hunt them down in this chaos. Luring the Voidworm away is still the best option.)
(It already absorbed my frequency once. Maybe I can get on its wavelength and draw its attention.)
Sorry, Professor Mornye, no time. I need the location of the Institute's anti-Voidworm defenses. I'm going to lure it there.
Got it. I'll find a way to draw the Voidworm there.
Noted.
Mornye, the Heliogyre is operational again. We can get the readings you need for the New Solar Ceremony.
But something happening out there isn't right.
Shaper, can you hear me?
Then everything is ready. Let's commence with the New Solar Ceremony!
(This is going to be interesting.)
